control_architecture: manual; pilot controls thrust via right-hand throttle and yaw via twist-grip nozzle deflection; pitch/roll by body lean
creator: Bell Aerosystems / U.S. Army Transportation Research Command
disclosure_citation: Bell Rocket Belt first untethered free flight 1961-04-20 at Niagara Falls, by Harold M. Graham; demonstrated to U.S. Army at Fort Eustis 1961-06-08. Documented in U.S. Army Transportation Research Command test reports and Bell Aerosystems engineering reports.
ip_citations: US Patent 3,243,144 (Wendell F. Moore, 'Personal Propulsion Unit'), filed 1962, issued 1966 — expired
prior_art_notes: The Bell Rocket Belt is the foundational disclosure of personal-scale VTOL flight. Wendell Moore's US Patent 3,243,144 has long expired, placing the basic personal rocket-belt architecture (twin-nozzle peroxide-decomposition, body-lean attitude control, hand-throttle thrust modulation) in the public domain. Establishes prior art for: (1) strap-on personal VTOL with cantilevered thrusters, (2) twist-grip vectoring for yaw, (3) body-lean attitude control. Anticipates: JetPack Aviation, Gravity Industries jet suit, Martin Aircraft Jetpack, Williams X-Jet, and any modern personal-VTOL claims relating to the strap-on form factor.
propulsion_details: Wendell Moore design: two stainless steel rocket nozzles fed by high-test peroxide (90% H2O2) decomposed over a silver catalyst pack; nozzles cantilevered behind the operator at shoulder height; thrust of approximately 280 lbf for ~21 seconds endurance.
sources: Moore, Wendell F. US Patent 3,243,144 'Personal Propulsion Unit', 1966.
Bell Aerosystems Rocket Belt program reports, NARA.
Carlson, Steve. Bell Rocket Belt: A History. The Rocket Belt Convention, multiple editions.

creator: Hawker Siddeley Aviation
disclosure_citation: Hawker Siddeley Harrier (production aircraft) first flight 1967-08-31; RAF squadron service 1969. The first operational vectored-thrust VTOL fighter in production. Documented in RAF archives, Hawker Siddeley company records, and the Smithsonian National Air and Space Museum collection.
ip_citations: GB Patents on Pegasus vectored-thrust nozzle, all expired
Hawker Siddeley Harrier patent estate, all expired
prior_art_notes: The Hawker Siddeley Harrier GR.1 establishes the production-scale vectored-thrust VTOL architecture — direct production descendant of P.1127 (1960). The RAF Harrier program (1969-2006) and U.S. Marine Corps AV-8 program (1971-2014) demonstrated multi-decade operational VTOL flight at production scale, comprehensively in the public domain. Hawker patents long expired. Anticipates F-35B and any modern vectored-thrust VTOL claim asserting novelty over the production-scale Pegasus four-poster nozzle architecture.
propulsion_details: Single Bristol Siddeley Pegasus 5 (later Mk.103) vectored-thrust turbofan with four rotatable nozzles (two cold-stream, two hot-stream); reaction-control bleed jets at airframe extremities.
sources: Mason, Francis K. Harrier. Patrick Stephens, 1986.
RAF archives, Hendon and Cosford.
Pegasus turbofan technical references, Rolls-Royce / Bristol Siddeley.

canonical_name: McDonnell Douglas / BAe AV-8B Harrier II
creator: McDonnell Douglas / British Aerospace / U.S. Marine Corps / Royal Air Force
disclosure_citation: AV-8B prototype YAV-8B first flight 1978-11-09 at McDonnell Douglas St. Louis (modified Harrier GR.1 airframe); production AV-8B first flight 1981-11-05; entered USMC service 1985. Joint U.S.-U.K. production program (RAF Harrier GR.5 / GR.7 / GR.9 variants). Documented in U.S. Navy / RAF technical orders, McDonnell Douglas / Boeing technical papers, and Smithsonian collections.
ip_citations: McDonnell Douglas / Boeing / BAe AV-8B patent estate, many filings 1975–2000, mostly expired
Pegasus 11 turbofan patents, all expired
prior_art_notes: AV-8B Harrier II is the production-definitive vectored-thrust VTOL fighter — second-generation development of the P.1127/Harrier lineage with supercritical wing and lift-improvement devices that nearly doubled payload over Harrier GR.1. The U.S. Marine Corps operated AV-8B from 1985 through 2014 (replaced by F-35B), and the RAF operated GR.5/7/9 variants 1989-2010 — comprehensive multi-decade operational disclosure of the production-scale Pegasus four-poster vectored-thrust architecture. Most McDonnell Douglas / Boeing AV-8B-era patents have now expired (filed 1975-2000). Combined with hawker-p-1127 (1960), hawker-siddeley-harrier (1967), and f-35b-lightning (2008), comprehensively places vectored-thrust VTOL fighter architecture in 50+ years of public-domain prior art.
propulsion_details: Single Rolls-Royce Pegasus Mk.105 (F402-RR-406) vectored-thrust turbofan with four rotatable nozzles; supercritical wing with leading-edge root extensions and lift-improvement devices (LIDs) added vs. P.1127/Harrier GR.1 to nearly double payload capacity.
sources: Mason, Francis K. Harrier. Patrick Stephens, 1986.
Markman and Holder. Straight Up. Schiffer, 2000.
U.S. Navy / U.S. Marine Corps AV-8B technical orders.
RAF Harrier GR.5/7/9 historical archives, RAF Museum.

creator: Bell Helicopter / Boeing / U.S. Marine Corps / U.S. Air Force
disclosure_citation: V-22 Osprey first flight 1989-03-19 at Bell Flight Research Center, Arlington TX; full operational capability declared 2007. Aircraft and engineering disclosures published exhaustively in NAVAIR / Marine Corps reports and in Norton's Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey volume.
ip_citations: US 5,209,429 (Bell, tilt-rotor cyclic/collective control)
US 5,310,138 (Bell, tilt-rotor wing fold)
Many additional Bell/Boeing V-22 patents, mostly issued 1985-2000, mostly expired
prior_art_notes: The V-22 Osprey is the production tilt-rotor that brought XV-15 prior art into operational service. Establishes prior art for: (1) production-grade triplex fly-by-wire tilt-rotor flight control, (2) cross-shafted prop-rotor drive with single-engine emergency operation in hover, (3) the operational conversion corridor with explicit nacelle-angle commanded transition. Bell's V-22-era tilt-rotor patents are largely now expired (most filed 1985–1995, 20-year terms). Together with XV-15, comprehensively places production tilt-rotor architecture in the public domain. Joby, Archer, and Vertical Aerospace's tilt-rotor claims are anticipated by V-22 disclosures where they overlap on architecture rather than electric-specific actuation.
propulsion_details: Two Rolls-Royce AE 1107C Liberty turboshafts (6,150 shp each) cross-shafted to drive 11.6 m three-blade gimbaled prop-rotors at wingtips.
sources: Norton, Bill. Bell-Boeing V-22 Osprey. Specialty Press, 2004.
NAVAIR V-22 Operational Test Reports, declassified portions.
Bell Helicopter V-22 patent filings, USPTO.

creator: Moller International / Paul Moller
disclosure_citation: Moller Skycar M200X (predecessor) first tethered hover 1989-04; M400 design publicly disclosed 1990 with continuous development through 2010s; first untethered M400 flight 2003-04 (limited tethered hover to 5 m). Moller holds the largest single inventor's portfolio of VTOL flying-car patents (>40 issued patents 1965-2015).
ip_citations: US Patent 5,115,996 (Moller, 'VTOL aircraft', 1992)
US 6,371,406, US 7,114,487, US 7,464,895, and many more — Moller patent portfolio comprising 40+ issued patents
prior_art_notes: Moller Skycar M400 is the foundational disclosure of the four-nacelle tilting-ducted-fan flying-car architecture. Although the M400 never achieved sustained free flight, Paul Moller's continuously-published patent portfolio (40+ issued US patents 1965-2015) constitutes the deepest single-inventor prior art base for VTOL flying-car designs. Establishes prior art for: (1) four-nacelle tilting-ducted-fan architecture, (2) Wankel rotary engine for VTOL propulsion, (3) clamshell thrust-vectoring deflectors. Most Moller patents have expired or are nearing expiration. Anticipates virtually every modern ducted-fan eVTOL (Lilium, AutoFlight, etc.) on architectural primitives.
propulsion_details: Eight Wankel rotary-engine-driven ducted fans in four tilting nacelles (two engines per nacelle); thrust vectoring via clamshell deflectors for transition between hover and cruise modes.
sources: Moller, Paul S. Multiple US patents 1965-2015, USPTO archive.
Moller, Paul. Skycar: The Air Car of the Future. Davis, 1996 (self-published).
Aviation Week and Popular Science coverage 1989-2010.

creator: Kaman Aircraft Corporation
disclosure_citation: Kaman K-MAX first flight 1991-12-23; FAA Type Certificate Part 27 (restricted category) granted 1994-08-30. Optionally-Piloted Aircraft (OPA) variant flown autonomously by U.S. Marine Corps in Afghanistan 2011-2014 (the first deployed unmanned heavy-lift helicopter).
ip_citations: Kaman K-MAX patent estate, multiple Kaman filings 1988–2010
prior_art_notes: Kaman K-MAX is the modern descendant of HH-43 intermeshing-rotor architecture and the first heavy-lift helicopter deployed for unmanned cargo operations in combat (Afghanistan, 2011-2014). Establishes prior art for: (1) modern certified intermeshing-rotor helicopter (extends HH-43 prior art into the 1990s-2010s with documented operational performance), (2) optionally-piloted heavy-lift cargo VTOL (Lockheed Martin retrofit), (3) Part 27 certification basis for intermeshing rotor.
propulsion_details: Two intermeshing rotors descended from HH-43 architecture; Honeywell T5317-A turboshaft (1,800 shp); cargo-only single-pilot configuration with skewed-axis intermeshing rotors providing 2,720 kg external lift capacity. Optionally-piloted retrofit (Lockheed Martin partnership).
sources: Kaman Aerospace press releases and FAA TC dossier 1994.
U.S. Marine Corps K-MAX OPA mission reports, declassified excerpts.
Lockheed Martin / Kaman OPA program technical materials.

control_architecture: triplex fly-by-wire descended from V-22 program; pressurized cabin for FL250 operations
creator: Bell Helicopter / Agusta / AgustaWestland / Leonardo S.p.A.
disclosure_citation: Bell/Agusta BA609 first flight 2003-03-06 at Bell Flight Research Center, Arlington TX (joint US-Italian program); program transferred to AgustaWestland 2011, renamed AW609; type certification target FAA Part 29 civil tilt-rotor with anticipated TC 2025-2026. Multiple Bell and Leonardo patents publicly filed.
ip_citations: Multiple Bell + Leonardo patents in the AW609 family
FAA TC application Part 29 civil tilt-rotor
prior_art_notes: The AW609 is the world's first civil-certified tilt-rotor aircraft, intended to enter service circa 2026. Direct architectural descendant of Bell XV-15 and V-22 Osprey, scaled and adapted for civil Part 29 operation with a pressurized cabin. Establishes Italian/European industrial prior-art lineage for civil tilt-rotor commercialization. Joby S4, Archer Midnight, and other commercial eVTOL tilt-rotor claims are anticipated by AW609's documented design and certification basis.
propulsion_details: Two Pratt & Whitney Canada PT6C-67A turboshafts (1,940 shp each) cross-shafted to drive 7.92 m three-blade gimbaled prop-rotors at wingtips; pressurized cabin (FL250 capability).
sources: Leonardo Helicopters AW609 type certification basis filings, FAA.
Vertical Flight Society Forum Bell/Agusta and Leonardo papers 2003–2024.
AgustaWestland / Leonardo press releases 2003–2024.

creator: Bell Helicopter Textron / U.S. Coast Guard / U.S. Army
disclosure_citation: Bell Eagle Eye first hover 2003-03-06; first transition flight 2003-08-22; full-scale TR918 unveiled at HAI Heli-Expo 2005. U.S. Coast Guard Deepwater program cancellation 2007 ended production but Bell continues design work. Documented in Bell technical papers and Coast Guard procurement records.
ip_citations: Bell V-22 / Eagle Eye patent estate
prior_art_notes: Bell Eagle Eye is the unmanned-scale tilt-rotor descended from the V-22 program — establishes prior art for: (1) tilt-rotor architecture in unmanned tactical UAV scale, (2) autonomous tilt-rotor mission operation. Together with bell-xv-15, v-22-osprey, bell-v-280-valor, and aerovironment-skytote, places tilt-rotor architecture across the full scale spectrum from small unmanned to large troop transport in comprehensive prior-art coverage.
propulsion_details: Twin tilting prop-rotors descended from XV-15 / V-22 design lineage but in unmanned scale; 9.45 m span; Pratt & Whitney Canada PW207E turboshaft.
sources: Bell Helicopter Eagle Eye technical papers, AHS Forum 2003–2008.
U.S. Coast Guard Deepwater program records, declassified portions.

creator: Schiebel Aircraft GmbH
disclosure_citation: Schiebel Camcopter S-100 publicly disclosed April 2005; entered service with Royal Australian Navy 2010 and Italian Navy 2011; deployed by 30+ naval forces worldwide. One of the most-deployed naval VTOL UAVs in history. Documented in Schiebel technical materials and naval procurement records.
ip_citations: Schiebel Aircraft patent estate, Austrian and EU filings
prior_art_notes: Schiebel Camcopter S-100 is the most-deployed naval VTOL UAV in the world (30+ navies, 30,000+ hours of operational flight). Establishes Austrian industrial prior-art lineage for autonomous shipborne VTOL UAS. Although conventional single-rotor architecture (not eVTOL specifically), the operational disclosure of autonomous shipboard VTOL operations is comprehensive and applicable to eVTOL claims around naval deployment.
propulsion_details: Single main rotor + small tail rotor (conventional helicopter architecture) in unmanned scale; rotary engine (Wankel); designed for shipborne operations including small-deck takeoff/landing in high sea state.
sources: Schiebel Aircraft GmbH press releases 2005-2024.
Royal Australian Navy / Italian Navy procurement records.

creator: UMS Skeldar (Saab AB / UMS Aero joint venture)
disclosure_citation: Saab Skeldar (originally Saab IsoMatic / Saab AERTEC) first flight 2007; in service with Spanish Armada 2018 and German Bundeswehr 2020. UMS Skeldar joint venture established 2015. Documented in Saab and UMS technical materials and naval procurement disclosures.
ip_citations: Saab AB / UMS Skeldar patent estate, Swedish and EU filings
prior_art_notes: Saab Skeldar V-200 is Sweden's lead naval VTOL UAV — competitor to Schiebel Camcopter S-100 with similar architectural pattern but Swedish industrial provenance. Adds Saab as Swedish defense industrial prior-art anchor alongside jetson-one (Sweden consumer eVTOL).
propulsion_details: Single main rotor + tail rotor unmanned helicopter; heavy-fuel rotary engine for naval-grade jet-fuel operation; designed for shipboard launch and recovery. Modular sensor payloads.
sources: Saab AB and UMS Skeldar press releases 2007-2024.
Spanish Armada and German Bundeswehr procurement records.

creator: Karem Aircraft Inc (Lake Forest, California)
disclosure_citation: Karem Aircraft AR40 / Optimum Speed Tilt-Rotor concept publicly disclosed in U.S. Army FVL program documentation 2008-2010; AR40 design materials in DARPA / Army Heavy Lift program filings. Karem holds Optimum Speed Rotor patents covering variable-RPM rotor operation.
ip_citations: Karem Aircraft Optimum Speed Rotor patents, multiple US filings
US Patents on slowed-rotor technology
prior_art_notes: Karem AR40 establishes prior art for: (1) variable-RPM Optimum Speed Rotor technology (continuously varying rotor RPM with forward speed and altitude for fuel-burn optimization), (2) very-large-payload tilt-rotor architecture. Karem's slowed-rotor patents are cited as prior art in Joby and Archer patent filings around variable-RPM rotor operation. Anticipates modern eVTOL claims around variable-RPM operation for efficiency optimization.
propulsion_details: Tilt-rotor architecture with Optimum Speed Rotor (OSR) technology — rotor RPM continuously varies between 60–100% of nominal as a function of forward speed and altitude, enabling significant fuel-burn reduction. Two large prop-rotors at wingtips.
sources: Karem Aircraft technical white papers and patent estate.
DARPA / Army Heavy Lift Tilt-Rotor program reports.
Karem, Abraham, et al. Karem Aircraft U.S. patent filings.

creator: Lockheed Martin / U.S. Department of Defense
disclosure_citation: F-35B first flight 2008-06-11 at Fort Worth; first hover 2010-03-18; first vertical landing 2010-03-18. X-35B (precursor) demonstrated STOVL 2001-07-07. Lockheed Martin and DoD program reports widely published; 3BSM and lift-fan architectures documented in many Pratt & Whitney and Rolls-Royce papers.
ip_citations: US 6,732,529 (Rolls-Royce / Bevilaqua, lift-fan)
US 5,209,428 and many related (3BSM lineage)
prior_art_notes: The F-35B is the production STOVL fighter that descends from Hawker P.1127 (vectored thrust), Yak-141 (3BSM), and the X-35B JSF demonstrator. Establishes prior art for: (1) clutched lift-fan gearbox driven by main engine shaft (lift-fan disengages for cruise), (2) production-grade 3BSM aft nozzle, (3) integrated 3BSM + lift-fan + roll-post hover trim system. The Bevilaqua lift-fan patent (US 6,732,529) is set to expire in 2019–2020 (filed 1999). After expiration, the lift-fan-with-clutch architecture is in the public domain — relevant for any future eVTOL claiming similar clutched-cruise-fan arrangements.
propulsion_details: One Pratt & Whitney F135-PW-600 (40,500 lbf) main turbofan with three-bearing swivel module (3BSM) aft nozzle; one Rolls-Royce LiftFan (clutched, two-stage counter-rotating fan) driving 18,000 lbf of cold thrust forward of cockpit; two roll-post nozzles in wing roots.
sources: Bevilaqua, Paul. 'Genesis of the F-35 Joint Strike Fighter.' Journal of Aircraft, Vol 46, 2009.
Pratt & Whitney F135 propulsion system technical papers.
Rolls-Royce LiftSystem technical papers.
Lockheed Martin F-35 program disclosures.

control_architecture: manual; pilot uses joysticks linked to fan cyclic pitch and engine throttle
creator: Martin Aircraft Company (Christchurch, New Zealand)
disclosure_citation: Martin Jetpack publicly unveiled 2008-07-29 at AirVenture Oshkosh, US; FAA approved as 'experimental category' aircraft 2010-08-22; first untethered piloted free-flight 2011-04-29. Glenn Martin holds NZ and US patents on the ducted-fan dual-rotor jetpack architecture.
ip_citations: NZ Patent 539537 (Martin, 2009)
US Patent 7,484,687 (Martin, 'Personal flight vehicle')
Multiple Martin Aircraft patents 2005–2018
prior_art_notes: Martin Jetpack is New Zealand's foundational disclosure of long-endurance personal-VTOL flight. Architecturally distinct from Bell Rocket Belt (rockets, 21 sec) and Gravity Industries (turbojets, 10 min) by using twin large ducted fans for ~30 min endurance. Establishes prior art for: (1) twin-ducted-fan personal-VTOL strap-on, (2) cyclic-pitch fan attitude control distinct from differential thrust, (3) FAA experimental-category personal jetpack certification basis. Glenn Martin's patents (NZ 539537, US 7,484,687) cover the architecture; some now expired.
propulsion_details: Two large ducted lift fans driven by a single 200 hp two-stroke V4 petrol engine; pilot strap-on; cyclic-pitch fans for attitude. Distinct from rocket-belt-class jetpacks by using ducted fans rather than turbojets/rockets — gives long endurance (~30 min).
sources: Martin, Glenn. NZ Patent 539537, 2009.
Martin, Glenn. US Patent 7,484,687, 2009.
Martin Aircraft Company press releases and SEC filings (NZX, 2015 IPO).

control_architecture: rider lean-to-bank with electronic stability augmentation; differential rotor thrust for attitude
creator: Aerofex Corporation (Manhattan Beach, California)
disclosure_citation: Aerofex first concept demonstrator publicly unveiled October 2008; Aero-X full-scale prototype publicly demonstrated 2014-04-30. Founded by Mark De Roche, ex-Boeing/NASA; Aerofex hold patents on ducted-rotor stability augmentation.
ip_citations: US 8,083,173 (De Roche, 'Air vehicle and method for operation thereof')
prior_art_notes: Aerofex Aero-X is the canonical disclosure of the modern hover-bike architecture: rider-straddle platform with twin large lift rotors fore and aft. Establishes prior art for: (1) personal hover-bike form factor, (2) intuitive lean-to-bank stability control mapping rider body input to differential rotor thrust, (3) the open-rotor straddle motorcycle-like operational paradigm. Anticipates Hoversurf S3, JetPack Aviation Speeder, and any subsequent hover-bike patent attempt.
propulsion_details: Two large open-rotor lift fans fore and aft of the rider straddle platform; combustion engine drives both rotors via mechanical transmission. Aerofex's stability-augmentation control provides intuitive lean-to-bank rider input similar to a motorcycle.
sources: De Roche, Mark. US Patent 8,083,173, 2011.
Aerofex press releases and demonstration materials 2008–2017.
Popular Science and Wired coverage 2010–2014.

creator: Urban Aeronautics Ltd (Yavne, Israel)
disclosure_citation: Urban Aeronautics' X-Hawk / AirMule first untethered hover 2009-12-21 at Megiddo airfield, Israel; CityHawk civil variant unveiled 2018-02-08. Urban Aeronautics holds the Fancraft / fuselage-internal-rotor patent family (US 7,789,342 et seq., filed early 2000s).
ip_citations: US 7,789,342 (Yoeli, 'VTOL aircraft having deployable wings')
US 8,302,903 and many additional Urban Aeronautics filings 2002–2020
prior_art_notes: Urban Aeronautics CityHawk / Cormorant establishes Israeli prior-art lineage for ducted-fan internal-rotor VTOL ('Fancraft'). The fuselage-internal lift-fan architecture is uncommon and relatively narrowly patented by Urban Aeronautics. Cormorant (military cargo variant) completed Israeli Defense Forces evaluation 2016. Establishes prior art for: (1) fuselage-internal ducted lift fan, (2) cascade-vane deflection for attitude control in lieu of differential rotor thrust, (3) compact urban-VTOL form factor without exposed rotors. Anticipates modern claims around safety-margin-improvement via internal rotors.
propulsion_details: Two large ducted lift fans embedded inside the fuselage (forward and aft of cabin); Vane Control System (VCS) cascade vanes deflect lift-fan exhaust for attitude control. Two smaller pusher propellers aft for cruise. Combustion-engine driven.
sources: Yoeli, Rafi. US Patent 7,789,342, 'VTOL Aircraft Having Deployable Wings,' 2010.
Urban Aeronautics technical white papers and press releases 2009–2020.
Israel Aerospace Industries / IDF Cormorant evaluation reports (declassified excerpts).

control_architecture: differential-thrust attitude allocation, sidestick pilot input, fly-by-wire stabilization
creator: e-volo GmbH (later Volocopter GmbH)
disclosure_citation: e-volo / Stephan Wolf, Thomas Senkel, Alexander Zosel: first manned multicopter flight, 2011-10-21 at Karlsruhe-Forchheim airfield; documented in DLR aviation news and academic press.
ip_citations: DE Patent DE102010032217 / WO2011050822 (Multicopter aircraft, Volocopter)
prior_art_notes: The e-volo VC1 is the first manned electric multicopter to achieve flight, predating EHang, Joby S2/S4, and Volocopter VoloCity. Establishes prior art for: (1) electric manned multicopter passenger air-mobility architecture, (2) sixteen-rotor circular lift configuration with differential-thrust attitude control, (3) the underlying Volocopter patent family (DE102010032217 and continuations). Anticipates virtually all current commercial electric multirotor air-taxi claims (EHang EH216, Volocopter VoloCity, Hyundai Supernal multirotor configurations).
propulsion_details: Sixteen brushless DC motors driving fixed-pitch propellers in a circular arrangement; lithium-polymer battery pack; differential-thrust attitude control.
sources: Senkel, T., Zosel, A., Wolf, S. e-volo flight test reports, 2011.
Volocopter GmbH patent family, esp. DE102010032217.
DLR magazine coverage 2011–2012 of first manned multicopter flight.

aliases: PAL-V Personal Air and Land Vehicle
creator: PAL-V International B.V. (Raamsdonksveer, Netherlands)
disclosure_citation: PAL-V One prototype first flight 2012-04-01; PAL-V Liberty production design unveiled 2017-03-07 at Geneva Motor Show; received EASA Type Certificate Part 27 (CS-27) for autogyro mode 2024-04-29. PAL-V founded 2008 by John Bakker.
ip_citations: PAL-V patent estate, Dutch / EU / PCT filings 2008–2024
prior_art_notes: PAL-V Liberty is the Netherlands' lead drive+fly transformer — architecturally distinct from Slovak peers (Klein/AeroMobil) by using autogyro lift rather than fixed-wing flight. Holds EASA Type Certificate Part 27 (autogyro mode), 2024-04-29 — the world's first type-certified flying car under autogyro classification. Direct architectural descendant of Cierva autogyro (1923) in road-legal passenger form factor.
propulsion_details: Two Rotax 912iS gasoline engines (one for road drive, one for flight); two-blade autorotating main rotor for lift in autogyro mode; pusher propeller aft for forward thrust; rotor folds for road operation. Cierva-style autogyro lineage in road-legal form factor.
sources: PAL-V press releases 2008–2024.
EASA Type Certificate Part 27, 2024-04-29 (PAL-V Liberty).
PAL-V technical white papers and patent filings.

control_architecture: decentralized self-organizing flocking; GPS localization; inter-drone communication; choreographed formation light shows
creator: CollMot Robotics / CollMot Entertainment (Budapest, Hungary) / Gábor Vásárhelyi / Tamás Vicsek
disclosure_citation: CollMot Robotics founded 2014 in Budapest by Gábor Vásárhelyi (with roots in Tamás Vicsek's collective-motion research group at Eötvös Loránd University); decentralized outdoor drone-swarm flight published in Vásárhelyi, G. et al. 'Optimized flocking of autonomous drones in confined environments,' Science Robotics 3(20), 2018. CollMot Entertainment performs commercial drone-light shows worldwide.
ip_citations: CollMot Robotics patents on decentralized drone-swarm coordination, Hungarian / EU filings
prior_art_notes: CollMot Robotics establishes Hungarian prior art for decentralized outdoor drone-swarm coordination — distinct from motion-capture-based indoor swarms (UPenn nano-quad, Intel Shooting Star) by operating outdoors at scale with onboard GPS and decentralized self-organizing flocking. Rooted in Tamás Vicsek's foundational collective-motion physics (the Vicsek model, 1995). The Science Robotics 2018 paper (Vásárhelyi et al.) is a comprehensive disclosure. Adds Hungary to the eVTOL / aerial-robotics prior-art map. Together with kmel-nano-quadrotor-swarm and intel-shooting-star, places multi-drone coordinated-fleet control in continuous prior-art coverage.
propulsion_details: Fleet of GPS-localized outdoor quadrotors flying decentralized self-organizing flocking patterns and choreographed light-show formations; distinct from motion-capture-based indoor swarms by operating outdoors at scale with onboard GPS and inter-drone communication. Hungarian research lineage from Tamás Vicsek's foundational collective-motion physics work.
sources: Vásárhelyi, G., Virágh, C., Somorjai, G., Nepusz, T., Eiben, A.E., Vicsek, T. 'Optimized flocking of autonomous drones in confined environments.' Science Robotics 3(20), 2018.
Vicsek, T. et al. 'Novel type of phase transition in a system of self-driven particles.' Physical Review Letters 75(6), 1995.
CollMot Robotics / CollMot Entertainment technical materials.

creator: H3 Dynamics (Singapore) / H3 Dynamics SAS (France)
disclosure_citation: H3 Dynamics founded 2014-07 in Singapore by Taras Wankewycz; hydrogen fuel-cell UAS commercialized starting 2019; H3 Dynamics demonstrated long-endurance hydrogen UAS at Singapore Airshow 2022 and 2024.
ip_citations: H3 Dynamics patent estate, Singapore / French / EU filings
prior_art_notes: H3 Dynamics establishes Singapore prior art for hydrogen-fuel-cell VTOL UAS. The Singapore/France dual-headquarters structure is increasingly common for aerospace startups operating across multiple regulatory regimes. Adds Singapore to the eVTOL OEM map and establishes hydrogen-electric VTOL architecture prior art alongside AMSL Vertiia (AU passenger-class hydrogen).
propulsion_details: Multi-rotor electric quadrotor airframe powered by hydrogen fuel cell (distinguishing H3 Dynamics from battery-electric peers). Singapore industrial design and integration with French subsidiary in Paris for European market penetration.
sources: H3 Dynamics press releases 2014-2024.
Singapore Airshow 2022 and 2024 exhibitor materials.

creator: Sikorsky Aircraft (Lockheed Martin)
disclosure_citation: Sikorsky S-97 Raider first flight 2015-05-22 at West Palm Beach FL. Production-track development of the X2 demonstrator (2008-08-27 first flight). Documented in Sikorsky technical papers and AHS / VFS Forum publications.
ip_citations: Sikorsky X2 / S-97 / Defiant patent estate, multiple US filings 2005–2024
prior_art_notes: Sikorsky S-97 Raider is the production-track sibling to the SB>1 Defiant — same X2 rigid-coaxial-rotor + rear-pusher architecture, smaller scale for armed-reconnaissance role. Together with sikorsky-boeing-defiant, comprehensively documents Sikorsky's modern coaxial rigid-rotor compound rotorcraft architecture in production-track form.
propulsion_details: Coaxial counter-rotating rigid main rotors (descended from X2) with rear pusher propeller; rigid rotor blades with active vibration control allow Advanced Blade-Tip Mach Number (ABMC) operation past conventional retreating-blade-stall speed limits.
sources: Sikorsky Aircraft press releases and technical papers 2015–2024.
AHS / VFS Forum papers from Sikorsky engineering 2008–2024.

control_architecture: single-operator centralized control of N-drone fleet; GPS localization; choreography-software fleet trajectory generation; the fleet flies as one controlled system
creator: Intel Corporation
disclosure_citation: Intel Shooting Star drone-light-show system publicly demonstrated 2016-11-04 (500-drone Guinness World Record, Krailling Germany); subsequently scaled to 1,218 drones (PyeongChang Olympics opening ceremony 2018-02-09) and 2,018+ drones (Folsom CA, 2018-07). Intel filed multiple patents on swarm light-show choreography and coordinated multi-drone control. Publicly disclosed via Intel press materials and Guinness World Records.
ip_citations: Intel patent estate on drone-swarm light shows and coordinated multi-drone control, multiple US filings 2015-2020

Establishes prior art for: (1) one operator controlling a fleet of N independent multirotors as a single coordinated system, (2) choreography-software-driven fleet trajectory generation at scale, (3) the 'fleet flies as one system' control paradigm directly relevant to any modular / swarm-coordinated eVTOL claim. Together with kmel-nano-quadrotor-swarm (2012) and collmot-drone-show (2014), comprehensively places coordinated-multi-drone control in 2012-2018 prior art across US academic, US commercial, and Hungarian commercial lineages.
propulsion_details: Fleet of purpose-built lightweight quadrotors (~330 g, foam-and-plastic, single onboard LED) flown in tightly-choreographed 3D formations from a single ground-station operator; GPS localization with centralized show choreography software ('one operator controls the fleet'). The Shooting Star Mini variant (~12 g, indoor) followed.

Guinness World Records, 'Most unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) airborne simultaneously,' 2016-11-04 and 2018-07.
PyeongChang 2018 Olympic Winter Games opening ceremony, 2018-02-09.

control_architecture: autonomous; capsule transfers between ground and air modules; air module is a coaxial-octorotor lifting the docked capsule
creator: Airbus / Italdesign / Audi
disclosure_citation: Airbus Pop.Up modular ground-and-air mobility concept unveiled 2017-03-07 at the Geneva International Motor Show, by Airbus and Italdesign (Volkswagen Group / Audi subsidiary); Pop.Up Next (adding Audi as full partner) unveiled 2018-03-06 at Geneva. Documented in Airbus and Italdesign press materials and design patents. The system is a passenger capsule that docks to either a ground-vehicle skateboard module or a quadrotor air module.

prior_art_notes: Airbus Pop.Up / Pop.Up Next (Airbus + Italdesign + Audi, 2017-2018) is the foundational commercial disclosure of the modular-docking passenger-mobility concept: a passenger capsule that docks to either a ground-vehicle module or an air module (a large coaxial-octorotor frame), with the air module lifting the docked capsule mid-journey. Establishes prior art for: (1) modular passenger-capsule + interchangeable-propulsion-module architecture, (2) in-journey transfer of a passenger pod between ground and air carriers, (3) the multi-modal modular-mobility thesis. Together with xpeng-aeroht-modular (2024), bell-apt (2018), and the academic modular-aerial-robot lineage (eth-distributed-flight-array, upenn-modquad), comprehensively places modular-docking eVTOL architecture in commercial and academic prior art from 2010 forward.
propulsion_details: Modular three-piece system: (1) a passenger capsule (cabin pod), (2) a ground-vehicle 'skateboard' module with road wheels, (3) an air module — a large coaxial-octorotor frame. The capsule docks to whichever module is appropriate for the trip leg; the air module lifts the capsule away from the ground module mid-journey. Battery-electric throughout. The modular-docking passenger-mobility concept.
sources: Airbus press release, 'Pop.Up: the modular ground-and-air passenger concept vehicle system,' 2017-03-07.
Italdesign / Audi Pop.Up Next materials, Geneva Motor Show 2018.
Airbus / Italdesign Pop.Up design patents, EUIPO records.

control_architecture: manual; pilot uses arm-mounted thrusters as control surfaces, body-leans for attitude
creator: Gravity Industries Ltd / Richard Browning
disclosure_citation: Gravity Industries Daedalus Mark 1 jet suit publicly demonstrated by Richard Browning 2017-04-20 (TED Talk, Vancouver); set Guinness world record fastest body-controlled jet suit 2019-11-12 (85.06 mph). Multiple subsequent public displays 2017–2024.

Filed against any modern personal-flight patent attempt asserting novelty over body-mounted multi-turbojet architecture, this entry combined with bell-rocket-belt is anticipating prior art.
propulsion_details: Five Jetcat P400 micro-turbojet engines (two on each forearm, one mounted on backpack); kerosene-fueled gas turbines with combined thrust approximately 1,100 N. Pilot controls thrust split via finger triggers and attitude via arm and body positioning.

Browning, Richard. Taking on Gravity. Bantam Press, 2019.
Gravity Industries press materials and patent filings.
Guinness World Records archive 2019-11-12.

Bell V-280 patent estate, USPTO records.

SkyDrive founded 2018-12 (incorporation date), spinning out from CARTIVATOR volunteer eVTOL project (founded 2012).
ip_citations: SkyDrive Japan patent filings, JP and PCT applications 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: SkyDrive SD-05 is Japan's lead passenger eVTOL, with Japan Civil Aviation Bureau type-certification application accepted 2021-10. The 12-motor coaxial-pair architecture is similar to EHang EH216-S; the program establishes Japanese prior-art lineage for multirotor passenger eVTOL and the JCAB SC-VTOL-equivalent certification approach. Originated in the volunteer CARTIVATOR project (2012), one of the earliest organized open-development eVTOL efforts.
propulsion_details: Twelve coaxial-pair lift motors mounted on six arms (six coaxial pairs); lithium-ion battery pack.
sources: SkyDrive Inc press releases 2018–2024.
Japan Civil Aviation Bureau type-certification application docket.
Toyota Motor Corporation investor disclosures (Toyota is a SkyDrive backer).

control_architecture: triplex fly-by-wire; coordinated nacelle tilt and prop-pitch transition control; simplified vehicle operations (SVO) intent
creator: Joby Aviation
disclosure_citation: Joby S4 unveiling and first flight: Joby first publicly disclosed S4 prototype 2018-12-20 (Joby press release); first untethered transition flight 2017-08 (predecessor S2); S4 Production Prototype first transition flight reported in Joby Aviation S-1 SEC filing 2020-08, full disclosure in 10-K filings 2021–2025.
ip_citations: US 10,994,841 (Bevirt et al., 'Aircraft hover stability')
US 11,377,217 (Bevirt et al., 'Vertical take-off and landing aircraft')
Multiple additional Joby patents in the 11,xxx,xxx series
prior_art_notes: The Joby S4 is the lead commercial tilt-rotor electric eVTOL. Direct architectural descendant of bell-xv-15 (1977) and v-22-osprey (1989) — Joby's tilt geometry, conversion corridor, and gimbaled prop-rotor topology are classical tilt-rotor architecture with electric distributed propulsion substituted for cross-shafted turboshaft. Establishes prior art for: (1) six-rotor tilt-rotor with stop-in-cruise props, (2) BLDC direct-drive tilt-rotor propulsion, (3) Part 23 SVO certification basis. Joby's patent estate (US 10,994,841 et seq.) covers specific implementations but is anticipated on the architectural level by XV-15 and V-22 disclosures.
propulsion_details: Six 5-blade variable-pitch tiltable propellers powered by independent BLDC motors; four wing-mounted (tilting) and two stabilator-mounted (tilting); each propeller stops in cruise to reduce drag.
sources: Joby Aviation S-1 (2020-08) and 10-K filings 2021–2025, SEC EDGAR.
Bevirt, JoeBen et al. US Patent 10,994,841 and US Patent 11,377,217.
Joby Aviation press releases 2018-12-20 (S4 unveil) and subsequent.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Joby engineering staff 2019–2024.

creator: Bell (Textron Inc)
disclosure_citation: Bell Nexus design publicly unveiled 2019-01-07 at CES Las Vegas; Nexus 4EX revised four-rotor variant unveiled 2020-01-07. Program wound down circa 2022; design materials and filings remain in the public record.
ip_citations: Bell / Textron Nexus patent estate, multiple US filings 2017–2022
prior_art_notes: Bell Nexus is one of the Uber-Elevate-era US passenger eVTOL concepts (2019). Although the program wound down, the public design disclosures remain prior art for: (1) hybrid-electric tilting-ducted-fan eVTOL architecture, (2) Bell's specific tilt-duct geometry distinct from Joby's open-rotor tilt-rotor approach. Establishes additional US prior-art lineage for tilt-duct passenger eVTOL (with bell-x-22 from 1966).
propulsion_details: Four (originally six) tilting ducted lift fans driven by a hybrid-electric powerplant (turbine-genset + battery + electric motors). Lateral wingtip and aft-mounted ducted-fan geometry.
sources: Bell / Textron press releases 2019–2022.
CES 2019 and CES 2020 Bell Nexus unveilings.
Bell Nexus patent filings, USPTO records.

creator: Elroy Air Inc (San Francisco)
disclosure_citation: Elroy Air founded 2016 by David Merrill and Kofi Asante; sub-scale Chaparral demonstrator publicly unveiled 2019-01-30; full-scale C1 first hover flight 2023-11-12 at Camarillo, CA. Air Force AFWERX / USAF Agility Prime contracts 2021-09 onward.
ip_citations: Elroy Air patent estate, multiple US and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Elroy Air Chaparral establishes prior art for: (1) hybrid-electric (turbine-genset + battery) cargo eVTOL, distinct from pure-battery designs by extending range to 500+ km, (2) modular swappable cargo-pod architecture decoupled from aircraft turnaround. Hybrid powerplant anticipates similar claims on series-hybrid eVTOL propulsion topology.
propulsion_details: Eight lift fans (four fore, four aft) plus four pusher propellers; turbine-electric hybrid genset (turbine-driven generator + battery + electric motor distribution). Modular cargo pod system swappable without aircraft contact.
sources: Elroy Air press releases and technical white papers 2019–2024.
USAF Agility Prime program disclosures.
FAA filings related to autonomous cargo operations.

creator: Sikorsky Aircraft (Lockheed Martin) / Boeing
disclosure_citation: Sikorsky-Boeing SB>1 Defiant first flight 2019-03-21 at West Palm Beach FL (U.S. Army Future Vertical Lift / Joint Multi-Role Technology Demonstrator program). Predecessor Sikorsky X2 first flight 2008-08-27. Sikorsky holds extensive coaxial-rigid-rotor patent estate.
ip_citations: Sikorsky X2 / Defiant patent estate, multiple US filings 2005–2024
U.S. Patents on rigid coaxial rotor technology
prior_art_notes: Sikorsky-Boeing Defiant establishes prior art for: (1) modern rigid coaxial counter-rotating rotor design overcoming retreating-blade-stall speed limits, (2) compound rotorcraft architecture (lift rotor + auxiliary cruise propulsion). Although not eVTOL, the architectural patterns directly anticipate any modern compound-rotorcraft eVTOL (Jaunt Journey, Karem AR40) claiming coaxial-plus-pusher novelty.
propulsion_details: Two coaxial counter-rotating rigid main rotors (descended from Sikorsky's X2 technology) for primary lift; rear pusher propeller for forward thrust. Rigid rotor design enables Advanced Blade-Tip Mach Number (ABMC) operation past conventional helicopter speed limits.
sources: Sikorsky / Boeing JMR-TD program disclosures.
Sikorsky X2 technology white papers, AHS / VFS Forums 2008–2024.
U.S. Army Future Vertical Lift program reports.

creator: Sabrewing Aircraft Company
disclosure_citation: Sabrewing Aircraft Rhaegal-A unveiled April 2019; sub-scale prototype hover trials 2020. Designed for 4,500 lb (2,040 kg) cargo capacity in unmanned configuration. Documented in Sabrewing technical materials and FAA filings.
ip_citations: Sabrewing patent estate, US filings 2018-2024
prior_art_notes: Sabrewing Rhaegal-A establishes US prior art for heavy-cargo (2-ton class) hybrid-electric eVTOL with ducted-fan architecture. Distinct from Elroy Air Chaparral by larger payload and ducted-fan rather than open-rotor lift. Anticipates other heavy-cargo eVTOL claims filed post-2019.
propulsion_details: Four ducted lift fans plus one pusher cruise fan; hybrid-electric powertrain with diesel genset for long-range operation.
sources: Sabrewing Aircraft technical materials 2019-2024.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions filings.

control_architecture: distributed control allocation across 36 fans, fly-by-wire
creator: Lilium GmbH
disclosure_citation: Lilium Jet 5-seat prototype unveiled and first hover flight 2019-05-16 at Manching; 7-seat production design unveiled 2021-03-30. Disclosures continued through Lilium SPAC merger Form S-4 2021-09 and subsequent NASDAQ filings until bankruptcy 2024-10.
ip_citations: Lilium GmbH patent estate including EP3401214, US10717543, multiple ducted-fan filings 2017–2023
prior_art_notes: Lilium Jet establishes prior art for the high-multiplicity ducted-fan-array eVTOL: 36 small embedded ducted fans tilting in flap-segment groupings to provide both lift and cruise. Architecturally a hyper-distributed extension of Bell X-22 (4 tilt-ducts) and Doak VZ-4 (2 tilt-ducts). Although Lilium filed for bankruptcy in October 2024, the patent estate (now in receivership / likely auctioned) covers specific implementations of the 36-fan ducted-array geometry and EASA SC-VTOL certification basis approaches. The fundamental architecture (multiple ducted fans in a tilting flap arrangement) is anticipated by Bell X-22 (1966).
propulsion_details: Thirty-six embedded electric ducted fans (24 on canard, 12 on main wing) tilting in flap segments; lithium-ion batteries; full DEP architecture with no separate cruise propulsor (ducted fans provide both lift and cruise thrust).
sources: Lilium Jet technical white paper 2021 (Munich, public download).
Lilium GmbH SPAC merger Form S-4 SEC filing 2021.
Wendel et al. 'Lilium Jet propulsion architecture' technical papers 2020–2022.

creator: Volocopter GmbH
disclosure_citation: VoloCity production-design unveiling 2019-08-14 at Helsinki ITS World Congress; precursor VC-200 first manned flight 2013-11-17; first public manned demo at Singapore 2019-10-22. EASA SC-VTOL certification process actively pursued; targeted operations Paris 2024 / 2026 commercial rollouts.
ip_citations: Volocopter GmbH patent estate, descended from DE102010032217
Multiple patents on multirotor airframe and DEP for passenger eVTOL
prior_art_notes: Volocopter VoloCity is the production design descending from e-volo VC1 (2011, first manned multicopter flight). 18-rotor circular-ring multirotor architecture, EASA SC-VTOL certification basis. Establishes the German prior-art lineage from e-volo through Volocopter for circular-ring multirotor passenger eVTOL — foundational and predates EHang in manned-flight precedence.
propulsion_details: Eighteen brushless DC motors driving fixed-pitch propellers around a 9.0 m circular ring; lithium-ion battery pack with redundant DC bus.
sources: Volocopter press releases 2013–2024.
EASA SC-VTOL filings, public docket.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Volocopter 2014–2024.

creator: Kitty Hawk Corporation / Larry Page
disclosure_citation: Kitty Hawk Heaviside publicly unveiled 2019-10-03 at TED Conference / Wired25 Festival; first manned flight 2019-09. Kitty Hawk Corporation (funded by Larry Page) ceased operations 2022-09-21. Documented in Kitty Hawk press releases and the public technical paper 'Heaviside: A Single-Pilot eVTOL Aircraft' (Vertical Flight Society, 2020).
ip_citations: Kitty Hawk Corporation patent estate, US filings 2015-2022 — assigned to various successor entities post-shutdown
prior_art_notes: Kitty Hawk Heaviside establishes US prior art for ultralight-class single-pilot tilt-rotor eVTOL with explicit low-noise design optimization. Although Kitty Hawk Corporation ceased operations 2022, the Heaviside disclosure is comprehensive — Larry Page-funded program with VFS Forum technical paper publication and Kitty Hawk's continued patent portfolio licensable through successor entities. Kitty Hawk also produced Cora (which became Wisk Aero after the Boeing JV, covered separately).
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors mounted on twin booms (four per side); lithium-ion battery; designed for very-low-noise (38 dB at 1500 ft) urban operation; FAA Part 103 ultralight category targeting.
sources: Kitty Hawk Corporation press releases 2019-2022.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 76 paper, 'Heaviside,' 2020.
TED Talk archive, Page/Kitty Hawk presentations 2018-2020.

control_architecture: redundant fly-by-wire
creator: Beta Technologies
disclosure_citation: Beta Technologies / Kyle Clark publicly unveiled Alia-250 design 2020-03-10; first hover flight 2020-05; first full transition flight 2021-04; multiple cross-country flights 2022–2024 publicly documented.
ip_citations: Beta Technologies patent estate 2018–2024 in CPC B64C 29/00 / B64D 27/24
prior_art_notes: Beta Alia-250 is the canonical commercial lift+cruise eVTOL: lift rotors stop and shut down in cruise, with a separate pusher providing forward propulsion. Architecturally simpler than tilt-rotor (no transition envelope coupling between lift and cruise), at cost of cruise drag from stopped lift rotors. Establishes prior art for: (1) production-scale lift+cruise architecture with mode-shutdown transition, (2) coaxial lift-pair geometry with single pusher cruise. Cited extensively by NASA Langley as the lift+cruise reference design.
propulsion_details: Four lift rotors mounted on dual booms (two coaxial pairs) for hover; single pusher propeller aft for cruise. All electric BLDC drive.
sources: Beta Technologies press releases 2020–2024.
Vertical Flight Society Forum technical papers from Beta 2021–2023.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions for VTOL filings.

creator: Manta Aircraft S.A. (Lugano, Switzerland / Varese, Italy)
disclosure_citation: Manta Aircraft ANN2 design publicly unveiled April 2020 at AERO Friedrichshafen; sub-scale prototype hover testing 2021. Manta Aircraft founded 2018 by Lucas Marchesini (Italian-Swiss aerospace engineer).
ip_citations: Manta Aircraft patent estate, Italian / Swiss / EU filings 2018-2024
prior_art_notes: Manta Aircraft ANN2 is the lead Italian-Swiss commercial eVTOL — hybrid-electric tilt-rotor with twin-fuselage geometry. Adds Italian prior-art lineage for hybrid-electric eVTOL alongside Leonardo AW609 (production tilt-rotor) and complements the Slovak (Klein Vision, AeroMobil) and Dutch (PAL-V) drive+fly Central European base.
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors and one pusher cruise propeller; hybrid-electric (combustion genset + battery) for ~600 km range. Twin-fuselage geometry distinct from competitors.
sources: Manta Aircraft press releases 2018–2024.
AERO Friedrichshafen 2020 unveiling materials.
EASA SC-VTOL engagement records.

creator: Manna Drone Delivery Ltd
disclosure_citation: Manna Drone Delivery Ltd founded 2018 in Dublin by Bobby Healy; operational food delivery service launched September 2020 in Oranmore, Galway; expanded to multiple Irish towns and UK 2021-2024. Documented in Irish Aviation Authority (IAA) operational approvals and Manna press materials.
ip_citations: Manna Drone Delivery patent estate, Irish / EU filings
prior_art_notes: Manna Air Delivery establishes Ireland's lead commercial drone-delivery OEM with operational deployment 2020-2024 across Irish towns. Distinctive: tethered cargo drop without landing (architectural variant of cargo delivery distinct from DJI FlyCart's set-down approach). Adds Ireland to the Western European eVTOL ecosystem.
propulsion_details: Quad-rotor multirotor optimized for low-altitude (< 80 m) urban delivery operations; lithium-ion battery; tethered cargo bay drops payload at delivery point without landing.
sources: Manna Drone Delivery press releases 2020-2024.
Irish Aviation Authority operational approvals public record.
Reuters and Irish Times coverage 2020-2024.

creator: Klein Vision s.r.o (Nitra, Slovakia)
disclosure_citation: Klein Vision AirCar Prototype 1 first flight test 2020-10; first inter-airport flight 2021-06-28 (Nitra to Bratislava). Slovak Transport Authority issued Certificate of Airworthiness 2022-01-26 — the world's first roadable-aircraft type certificate. Klein Vision founded by Stefan Klein.
ip_citations: Klein Vision patent estate, Slovak / EU / PCT filings 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: Klein Vision AirCar holds the world's first type-certified roadable-aircraft certificate (Slovak Transport Authority, 2022-01-26). Establishes Slovak prior-art lineage and certified-product anchor for the drive+fly transformer category. Not VTOL but architecturally adjacent — many drive+fly eVTOL designs (ASKA A5, AeroMobil 4.0) build on the same retractable-wing transformer architecture pioneered in road-legal aviation by Klein.
propulsion_details: Conventional fixed-wing aircraft with retractable wings that fold into rear fuselage; 1.6 L BMW engine drives both road wheels and rear pusher propeller; transitions from car to aircraft in 2 minutes 15 seconds via push-button mechanism. Not strictly VTOL — STOL — but establishes drive+fly transformer prior art.
sources: Klein Vision press releases 2020–2024.
Slovak Transport Authority Certificate of Airworthiness, 2022-01-26.
Klein, Stefan. AirCar technical white paper, 2021.

creator: Eve Holding Inc (Embraer S.A. spinoff)
disclosure_citation: Eve UAM Solutions launched as Embraer subsidiary 2020-10-08; spun off as standalone Eve Holding via SPAC merger 2022-05-09 (NYSE: EVEX). Aircraft design publicly unveiled 2022; first prototype unveiled 2024.
ip_citations: Eve / Embraer patent estate, multiple Brazilian, US, and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Eve Air Mobility is Brazil's lead commercial eVTOL, backed by Embraer's 100+-year aerospace heritage. The 8+1 lift+cruise architecture is architecturally similar to Beta Alia and AutoFlight Prosperity I; establishes Brazilian / Latin American prior-art lineage for commercial lift+cruise eVTOL. Eve has the deepest commercial aerospace certification experience among any eVTOL company by virtue of Embraer parentage.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors (four on each side, mounted on dual booms) for hover; single pusher propeller aft for cruise. Lithium-ion battery.
sources: Embraer / Eve Holding 20-F SEC filings 2022–2024.
Eve Air Mobility press releases 2020–2024.
ANAC (Agência Nacional de Aviação Civil) eVTOL certification framework filings.

creator: AMSL Aero Pty Ltd (Bankstown, Australia)
disclosure_citation: AMSL Aero Vertiia design publicly unveiled March 2021; sub-scale prototype hover testing 2022; first hover flight 2023-02-15. AMSL Aero founded 2017 by Andrew Moore (former CSIRO and Boeing engineer) and Siobhan Lyndon. Targeted at hydrogen-fuel-cell-powered eVTOL ambulance and regional connectivity missions.
ip_citations: AMSL Aero patent estate, Australian and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: AMSL Aero Vertiia is Australia's lead commercial eVTOL with a distinctive commitment to hydrogen-fuel-cell propulsion for 1,000 km range — addressing the energy-density limitation of pure-battery eVTOL. Establishes Australian prior-art lineage for hydrogen-eVTOL and for long-range regional eVTOL distinct from short-range urban air taxi. Anticipates other hydrogen-fuel-cell eVTOL claims (e.g., Joby's post-acquisition H2 program, Airbus ZEROe rotorcraft).
propulsion_details: Eight lift propellers and two pusher cruise propellers; hydrogen fuel cells provide electrical power (with backup battery) — distinct architectural commitment to hydrogen as primary energy source for range extension to 1,000 km.
sources: AMSL Aero press releases 2021–2024.
Australian Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) eVTOL framework filings.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers from AMSL.

control_architecture: triplex fly-by-wire with simplified vehicle operations
creator: Archer Aviation
disclosure_citation: Archer Aviation S-1 SEC filing 2021-06-10; Archer Midnight design publicly unveiled 2022-11-16; Maker demonstrator first hover flight 2021-12; first full transition 2023-06.
ip_citations: Archer Aviation patent estate, multiple filings 2020–2024 in CPC B64C 29/00
prior_art_notes: Archer Midnight is a hybrid lift+cruise / tilt-rotor architecture with six tilting and six lift-only propellers. Architecturally descends from the NASA GL-10 DEP tilt-wing concept and the XV-15 tilt-rotor, with the lift+cruise split providing lower hover-prop disk loading at the cost of cruise drag from idle lift props. Disclosures in SEC filings make this a commons-grade entry. Establishes prior art for: (1) 6+6 hybrid tilt-and-lift-only DEP architecture, (2) feathering cruise props with fixed-cruise disk.
propulsion_details: Twelve electric motors driving twelve propellers: six wing-leading-edge tilting propellers (function as lift in hover and cruise propulsion) and six aft-mounted lift-only propellers (stop and feather in cruise).
sources: Archer Aviation S-1 (2021-06-10) and 10-K filings 2022–2025, SEC EDGAR.
Archer Aviation press releases including 2022-11-16 Midnight unveil.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 technical paper from Archer engineering.

control_architecture: autonomous flight management with pilot override; differential-thrust attitude allocation
creator: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: XPENG Voyager X2 publicly unveiled 2021-07-16 at XPENG Tech Day; first untethered manned flight 2022-10-10 at Dubai. AeroHT subsequently unveiled X3 (2023) and the modular Land Aircraft Carrier (2024). AeroHT holds Chinese patents on coaxial multirotor and modular drive+fly architecture.
ip_citations: AeroHT / XPENG patent estate, multiple Chinese filings
prior_art_notes: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary) is one of three lead Chinese passenger eVTOL programs (with EHang and AutoFlight). The X2 establishes Chinese prior art for coaxial-pair multirotor passenger eVTOL with autonomous + pilot-override flight management. AeroHT's subsequent X3 and modular Land Aircraft Carrier (2024) extend to drive+fly transformer geometry.
propulsion_details: Eight motors in coaxial pairs (four arms × 2 rotors) on a closed-loop frame; lithium-ion battery; full autonomous + pilot-override flight control.
sources: XPENG Tech Day 2021-07-16 announcement materials.
Reuters and South China Morning Post coverage of 2022-10-10 Dubai flight.
AeroHT product disclosures and Chinese patent filings.

creator: Aerofugia (Geely Holding Group eVTOL subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: Geely Holding Group launched Aerofugia subsidiary 2021-09-14 in Chengdu; AE200 design publicly unveiled at Beijing Air Show 2022-02-10; sub-scale prototype hover 2023-04. Geely also acquired Terrafugia (US) 2017-11 and uses combined IP base for AE200.
ip_citations: Aerofugia / Geely patent estate, Chinese filings
Terrafugia (acquired) prior US patent estate
prior_art_notes: Geely Aerofugia AE200 is China's third major commercial passenger eVTOL (with EHang and AutoFlight). Distinctive: Geely's parallel ownership of Terrafugia (US flying car company since 2017) provides cross-Pacific patent integration. Establishes additional Chinese tilt-rotor prior-art lineage alongside TCab E20.
propulsion_details: Six tilting electric prop-rotors in a 4+2 configuration similar to Joby S4 and TCab E20; lithium-ion battery; lithium-ion solid-state battery development partnership with sister Geely brands.
sources: Aerofugia / Geely press releases 2021-2024.
Beijing Air Show 2022 unveiling materials.
Geely Holding Group investor disclosures.

creator: Airbus Helicopters (Donauwörth, Germany) / Airbus Defence and Space
disclosure_citation: Airbus CityAirbus NextGen design publicly unveiled 2021-09-21 at Airbus Summit; first scaled prototype hover 2024-09. EASA SC-VTOL type-certification process initiated 2022. Predecessor CityAirbus demonstrator first hover 2019-05-03.
ip_citations: Airbus Helicopters patent estate, multiple EU / US / PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Airbus CityAirbus NextGen is the production-track lift+cruise successor to the Vahana research program. Establishes German industrial prior-art lineage for production-scale lift+cruise passenger eVTOL with V-strut lift-rotor mounting geometry distinct from boom-mounted competitors (Beta Alia, Eve, Wisk). Airbus's industrial certification scale and EASA insider position make this a particularly important commons-grade entry.
propulsion_details: Eight lift propellers on V-shaped struts plus two pusher cruise propellers aft on a fixed-wing airframe; lithium-ion battery.
sources: Airbus Helicopters press releases 2019–2024.
Airbus Summit 2021 unveiling materials.
EASA SC-VTOL public docket.

creator: Vertical Aerospace Ltd (Bristol, United Kingdom)
disclosure_citation: Vertical Aerospace VX4 unveiled 2021-09-29 at SPAC announcement / investor presentation; first tethered hover 2022-09-26; first untethered transition 2024-12. SPAC merger with Broadstone Acquisition (NYSE: EVTL) completed 2021-12. Lead investors include Microsoft / Avolon / American Airlines / Honeywell / Rolls-Royce.
ip_citations: Vertical Aerospace patent estate, multiple UK, US, PCT filings 2019–2024
prior_art_notes: Vertical Aerospace VX4 is the United Kingdom's lead commercial eVTOL — the post-Hawker British vectored/tilt VTOL inheritance translated into electric propulsion. The 4+4 tilt-and-lift hybrid is architecturally between Joby S4 (6 tilt) and Archer Midnight (6+6 hybrid). Establishes UK prior-art lineage for commercial tilt-rotor eVTOL; Rolls-Royce propulsion subsystem involvement provides direct industrial-scale supplier coverage.
propulsion_details: Four wing-tip-mounted tilting electric propellers and four boom-mounted lift-only propellers; a 4+4 tilt-and-lift hybrid configuration. Lithium-ion battery; Honeywell flight controls; Rolls-Royce electric propulsion subsystems.
sources: Vertical Aerospace 20-F SEC filings 2021–2024.
Vertical Aerospace press releases including 2021-09-29 SPAC announcement.
Honeywell Avionics and Rolls-Royce eVTOL-program technical disclosures.

creator: Honda Motor Company / Honda Aircraft Company
disclosure_citation: Honda Motor Company eVTOL program publicly disclosed 2021-09-30 at 'Honda Dream Loop' announcement; design materials and target specifications subsequently disclosed in Honda investor briefings 2022–2024. Targeted commercial operation 2030.
ip_citations: Honda Motor Company eVTOL patent estate, Japanese / US filings
prior_art_notes: Honda eVTOL establishes prior art for hybrid-turbine-electric passenger eVTOL — Honda's specific architecture decision is to use a gas-turbine genset (leveraging HondaJet experience) for range extension. Anticipates other turbine-hybrid eVTOL claims (Elroy Air Chaparral, AMSL Aero Vertiia variants).
propulsion_details: Hybrid-electric powerplant with gas-turbine genset + battery (Honda leverages HondaJet turbine experience). Eight lift rotors and one or two cruise propellers. Distinctive commitment to gas-turbine-hybrid for 400+ km range, distinct from pure-battery short-range eVTOL.
sources: Honda 'Dream Loop' announcement, 2021-09-30.
Honda Motor Company investor disclosures 2021–2024.
Honda Aircraft Company technical materials.

creator: AIR Mobility Ltd (Pardes Hanna, Israel)
disclosure_citation: AIR ONE publicly unveiled 2021-10-04 in San Francisco; first untethered manned hover flight 2023-06-19 at AIR Pardes Hanna test site, Israel. AIR Mobility founded by Rani Plaut and Chen Rosen, 2018.
ip_citations: AIR Mobility patent estate, Israeli and PCT filings 2019–2024
prior_art_notes: AIR ONE is Israel's lead consumer-grade eVTOL — distinct from CityHawk (commercial ducted-fan) by addressing the personal-recreational segment. Lift+cruise architecture targeting Part 23 certification with consumer operability emphasis. Establishes Israeli prior-art lineage for consumer/recreational eVTOL distinct from passenger air taxi.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors (four on each of two booms) plus a single pusher for cruise; lithium-ion battery; designed to fold/store like a consumer-grade ultralight.
sources: AIR Mobility press releases 2021–2024.
Globes (Israeli business newspaper) coverage of 2023-06-19 flight test.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers from AIR engineering.

creator: AutoFlight (Shanghai)
disclosure_citation: AutoFlight Prosperity I publicly unveiled 2021-12-13; first untethered hover flight 2022-04; full-scale prototype transition flight 2023-02-23 at Kunshan; cross-Pearl River Delta demonstration flight Hong Kong → Macau 2024-04-29. AutoFlight founded by Tian Yu (former co-founder of EHang).
ip_citations: AutoFlight (Shanghai) patent estate, multiple Chinese filings 2018–2024
prior_art_notes: AutoFlight Prosperity I is the leading Chinese lift+cruise eVTOL — architectural sibling to Beta Alia-250 with eight rather than four lift rotors. The 250 km Hong Kong-Macau demonstration flight (2024-04-29) is the longest eVTOL flight publicly documented as of mid-2024. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for lift+cruise architecture and supports EASA SC-VTOL certification basis dual-track with EHang's CAAC certification.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors mounted on dual booms (four per side) for hover; single push propeller aft for cruise. Lithium-ion battery bus.
sources: AutoFlight press releases 2021–2024.
China Daily and Xinhua coverage of 2024-04-29 Hong Kong-Macau flight.
AutoFlight technical white papers (Chinese and English editions).

creator: The ePlane Company (IIT Madras spinout)
disclosure_citation: The ePlane Company e200 unveiled 2022-08; first sub-scale prototype hover flight 2022-12; full-scale prototype rolled out 2023-09 at IIT Madras Research Park. Founded 2017 by Prof. Satya Chakravarthy. Awarded Anjani Mashelkar Inclusive Innovation Award 2023.
ip_citations: ePlane Company / IIT Madras patent estate, Indian and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: ePlane e200 is India's lead commercial eVTOL — IIT Madras research spinout with explicit design constraint for compact urban Indian airspace. Establishes Indian prior-art lineage for commercial lift+cruise eVTOL and the design philosophy of compact-footprint operation in dense urban environments. Targeted at DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation, India) certification.
propulsion_details: Eight lift rotors mounted on twin booms for hover; single pusher for cruise; lithium-ion battery; specifically designed for the dense footprint of Indian urban airspace (compact rotor disc geometry).
sources: Chakravarthy, Satya. ePlane Company technical white papers, 2022–2024.
Indian DGCA eVTOL certification framework filings.
IIT Madras Research Park ePlane Company disclosures.

creator: Crisalion Mobility S.L. (Tecnalia Research and Innovation spinoff)
disclosure_citation: Crisalion Mobility Integrity design publicly unveiled 2022-09-08 at Madrid; sub-scale demonstrator hover 2023. Crisalion Mobility spun off from Tecnalia Research and Innovation (Basque Country research center). Documented in Crisalion press materials and Tecnalia research disclosures.
ip_citations: Tecnalia / Crisalion patent estate, Spanish and EU filings
prior_art_notes: Crisalion Mobility Integrity is Spain's lead commercial eVTOL — Tecnalia Research and Innovation (Basque Country) spin-off establishes Spanish industrial prior-art lineage for tilt-rotor distributed-electric passenger eVTOL. Adds Spain to the European eVTOL OEM map alongside Germany (Volocopter, Lilium, Wingcopter, Airbus CityAirbus), Italy (Leonardo, Manta), Netherlands (PAL-V), Slovakia (Klein Vision, AeroMobil), Czech Republic (Zuri), Sweden (Jetson).
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors in a coaxial-pair configuration on an X-frame; lithium-ion battery; modular passenger/cargo configurations.
sources: Crisalion Mobility press releases 2022-2024.
Tecnalia Research and Innovation public technical disclosures.

creator: TCab Tech (Shanghai TCab)
disclosure_citation: TCab Tech E20 sub-scale technology demonstrator first transition flight 2022-12-07 at Tianjin; full-scale E20 prototype unveiled 2023-09-13. TCab founded 2021 in Shanghai by Yon Wui Ng; technical leadership from former Volocopter and Lilium engineers.
ip_citations: TCab Tech patent estate, Chinese filings 2022–2024
prior_art_notes: TCab Tech E20 is China's lead tilt-rotor passenger eVTOL — architectural sibling to Joby S4 with the same 4+2 tilt-rotor configuration. Establishes Chinese-lineage prior art for the tilt-rotor electric eVTOL category, ensuring the architectural pattern is disclosed across at least four geographic prior-art bases (US, EU, China, Italy).
propulsion_details: Six tilting electric prop-rotors in a 4+2 configuration: four wing-mounted tilting and two stabilator-mounted tilting; lithium-ion battery.
sources: TCab Tech press releases and technical white papers 2022–2024.
Vertical Flight Society Forum 2023 papers including TCab Tech engineering.
South China Morning Post and Caixin coverage 2022–2024.

creator: SZ DJI Technology Co Ltd (Shenzhen)
disclosure_citation: DJI FlyCart 30 publicly unveiled 2023-08-16 in Shenzhen; CAAC type certification application 2023; commercial deliveries to Chinese and international markets 2024. DJI is the world's largest drone manufacturer and holds an extensive multirotor patent estate.
ip_citations: DJI patent estate, vast (>1000 patents) covering multirotor architectures, autopilot, motors
prior_art_notes: DJI FlyCart 30 is the entry of the world's largest drone manufacturer into the heavy-cargo eVTOL category. DJI's massive patent estate and operational scale (millions of multirotor flights/day globally) constitute foundational prior-art coverage for: (1) coaxial-pair multirotor manufacturing at scale, (2) autonomous multirotor cargo delivery, (3) consumer-priced multirotor architecture migration to commercial cargo class. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for heavy-payload multirotor cargo eVTOL with explicit Beyond Visual Line of Sight (BVLOS) operational basis.
propulsion_details: Four-axis eight-rotor coaxial-pair multicopter (four arms, two motors per arm); designed for 30 kg cargo capacity over 16 km range; lithium-ion battery.
sources: DJI press releases and product disclosures 2023–2024.
CAAC type certification dossier (DJI FlyCart 30).
DJI patent estate, USPTO and CNIPA records.

creator: Supernal LLC (Hyundai Motor Group eVTOL subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: Hyundai Supernal S-A2 unveiled at CES 2024 (Las Vegas), 2024-01-09. Predecessor S-A1 design unveiled at CES 2020 (2020-01-07). Supernal LLC incorporated under Hyundai Motor Group, headquartered in Washington DC with engineering in Irvine CA, but the design lineage and corporate ownership are South Korean.
ip_citations: Hyundai Motor Group / Supernal patent estate, multiple US, KR, and PCT filings
prior_art_notes: Hyundai Supernal S-A2 is Korea's lead commercial eVTOL program, backed by Hyundai Motor Group's automotive industrial scale. The 4+4 tilt-rotor configuration is architecturally between Joby S4 (4+2) and tilt-wing designs. Establishes Korean prior-art lineage for tilt-rotor commercial eVTOL; targets FAA Part 23 certification and 2028 commercial operation.
propulsion_details: Eight tilting electric prop-rotors arranged in a 4+4 configuration (four wing-mounted, four stabilator-mounted); battery-electric with Hyundai Mobis battery integration.
sources: Supernal CES 2024 press materials, 2024-01-09.
Hyundai Motor Group investor disclosures.
FAA Part 23 / Special Conditions for VTOL filings.

aliases: 小鹏汇天 陆地航母
creator: AeroHT (XPENG flying car subsidiary)
disclosure_citation: XPENG AeroHT Land Aircraft Carrier publicly unveiled 2024-04-04 at Beijing Auto Show; production target 2026. Combines a road-legal 6x6 ground vehicle ('Mothership') containing a fold-out manned eVTOL drone.
ip_citations: AeroHT / XPENG patent estate, multiple Chinese filings
prior_art_notes: XPENG AeroHT Land Aircraft Carrier is the most ambitious modular drive+fly transformer architecture publicly disclosed: a separate ground vehicle that carries and deploys a manned eVTOL. Establishes Chinese prior-art lineage for modular vehicle-eVTOL architectures, distinct from integrated transformer-eVTOL designs (ASKA A5, AeroMobil) where one vehicle physically transforms. Anticipates future modular-carrier patent filings.
propulsion_details: Modular two-component system: (1) road-legal 6x6 hybrid-electric ground vehicle that carries (2) a fold-out single-pilot multirotor eVTOL deployed from the rear of the vehicle. The eVTOL has six rotors in a deployable wing arrangement.
sources: XPENG AeroHT Beijing Auto Show 2024-04-04 unveiling materials.
AeroHT / XPENG technical disclosures 2024.
Reuters and South China Morning Post coverage 2024-04-04.
